name: Tracey Smith
most_valuable: Tips on different  tools to incorporate during lessons. Learning more about teaching special needs.
transportation_detail: Pick up from the overflow parking very quick & efficient
food_detail:
I was only there for the day & only had lunch & snacks. I got to the buffet a little late & there was barely anything left. 
There seemed to be plenty of snack although not altogether healthy. Never did find out where the bottled water & drinks were. For the people only there for a day I think that should've been explained upon arrival.
community_ideas: Maybe do an introductory of instructors attending for the day before the first session is about to begin. I felt those of us that only came for a day or two had to navigate in introductions. Not sure how that would work for someone that's ultra shy..
day_flow_detail: I felt the last session went a bit too long & also the Kaizen kids presentation. Would have preferred to spend more time on the adaptive aquatics.
no_brainer:
Location, pricing & content. 
I wouldn't want to share a room let alone a bed!!
belief_shift: How to teach specials needs, breaking it down. Simplifying steps & skills.
differentiation: I think the camaraderie. It was definitely a lively crowd.
suited_for: Any instructor trying to grow & learn.
anything_else: Better Wi-Fi access. Some of the videos the presenters had couldn't be shown due to connection issues. Also the acoustics were sketchy by the pool. Could be my age & my hearing not being the best, but I couldn't hear much of what Becca had to say.

name: Danielle Jacobson
most_valuable: So much time with so many other swim professionals!
valuable_session: They all were so amazing. The variety of ages, techniques, experience made it so helpful and I was able to learn so much
anything_else: You guys are all amazing! The experience was unmatched and I would love to help with planning the next one :)

name: Shalene Hill
most_valuable: Connection time with other swim instructors
food_detail:
We had such a beautiful indoor and outdoor kitchen, wondering if the catered can cook on site - 
What if there is a cook/caterer/chef in the community, someone related to a swim instructor, who could be in charge of running the kitchen.
They wouldn’t have to cook all the meals, they can make cooking/prep assignments and dish/clean up assignments.
They could come on work passes for thrmself and thier swim instructor…
valuable_session:
Sarah King - working with adults 
Her knowledge and insight are also very helpful for children
future_topics: I want to incorporate group singing throughout each day! Maybe with meals or as an end of day wrap up. The singing was so powerful!
community_ideas: The leadership team was working so hard, especially Kaz… wondering if there is a way to lighten that load.
belief_shift:
I like to think that I am teaching from and AND kind of place and not an OR kind of place:
Gentleness AND pushing boundaries
Nervous System Regulation AND survival skills
Playfulness AND effectiveness
differentiation:
The lasting connections
The opportunity to bring singing and ring in the way that I do.
suited_for:
Any swim instructor at any point on the journey 
It could potentially begin to include partners of swim I  who have a skill to add, like a chef or caterer
anything_else: Oh! I want everyone to have the opportunity to float next time!

name: Kristine Lee
most_valuable:
Staying at the house and being able to have conversations with instructors in a no pressure, comfy setting. Speakers and wrkshops were excellent - not overwhelming. 

I didn’t feel tired at the end of the day unlike other industry conferences. All the info was just enough so that we could recap each session from the day. 

Great planning of breaks in between.
food_detail: Fruits, cheese and cracker snacks. Thanks for the candy! I destroyed the m&m’s.
valuable_session:
We def got value out of all instructor presentations. 

Loved that the sessions were long enough that there was enough question and answer time.
unclear_topic: No. But I loved that if we had question we can easily speak with them in the house.
future_topics: Survival swim and diff variations of teachings, strategies, progressions and troubleshoots.
community_ideas: I think it’s pretty darn strong. The community was built outside the group and coming together strengthened the community!!!
hands_on_detail:
Don’t know how doable it is, if there was a mentor in the water-that would be awesome. Just like the breakout sesh. 

I’m sure there was super value in the breakout sesh that we missed with Becca and Jacob.  So I guess something like that. 

Next time we won’t leave on Sunday. LOL!
belief_shift:
Natalie brought the non physical side. 

Exposure to survival swim, underwater prob solving etc. 

There’s more to aquatic instruction than just strokes. Challenge the mind, fun and mindset.
differentiation: The community was welcoming, everyone cheered for each other, not info overload. The social aspect felt like a hangout rather than a stuffy retreat.
suited_for: Anyone that has their own swim biz that’s not one of the big boxers. Anyone who is growing their biz and making it better.
anything_else:
Thank you from the bottom of our hearts! (Marcus and Kristine). Truly one of the best experiences we had in our lives. 

Jacob and the team: the heart and intention you put into this was evident by how well the event turned out, the type of instructors the community attracts, and the overall energy we feel a part of. SHS is truly something special and we feel so blessed to have come across you guys.
